sources.label: GlobeNewswire - Allot at 46th William Blair Growth Stock Conference (June 2-4, 2026)
PR Newswire - Allot Q1 2026 results: revenue +14% YoY, SECaaS ARR $33.7M +59% YoY (May 12, 2026)
StockAnalysis - ALLT analyst consensus Strong Buy, avg PT $13.5

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental information since June 6 - only the William Blair conference appearance (June 2-4) and the already-priced Q1 beat from May 12. Today's -3.4% drift on no news deepens the YTD drawdown to -24.9% with the SECaaS/network-intelligence thesis intact, which marginally improves residual asymmetry but is not a sharp enough dislocation to move the rank versus yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: SECaaS revenue +71% YoY and ARR +59% YoY with a Tier 1 Tera III multimillion-dollar win, FY26 guide of $113-117M reiterated, and a $0.35B cap down ~25% YTD - a cheap, under-owned security/traffic-intelligence call option with consensus PT ~$13.5 implying ~65% upside.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is intact but unchanged; the stock is drifting lower on no news and the next real catalyst is Q2 results or a fresh SECaaS operator win, so the setup is a slow-burn re-rating rather than an imminent one.
verdicts.bear: Sub-$400M cap with legacy DPI revenue still masking the SECaaS mix shift; continued price bleed despite a Q1 beat suggests the market doubts conversion of the SECaaS pipeline, and small-cap Israeli tech carries persistent flow and geopolitical discounts.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New Tier 1 SECaaS operator launch or Q2 print showing SECaaS ARR sustaining ~60% growth against the reiterated FY26 guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: SECaaS ARR growth decelerates or legacy gateway revenue declines faster than security ramps, breaking the cybersecurity-first re-rating story.
